base_read._comment: Good proof: a Vacation (absence_type_id 300000071752546) entry in base table ANC_PER_ABS_ENTRIES starting on this run's prefix-derived date ${ABS_START_DASH} for seeded persons 6 and 9. These are long-tenured US1 employees (assignment start dates in the 2000s) with a published work schedule, so a near-future Monday validates as a scheduled workday. Keyed by person_number. The bad person (10) got an invalid absence type so no base entry exists for it -> proves absence.
base_read.sql: SELECT p.person_number AS PNUM, TO_CHAR(MAX(e.per_absence_entry_id)) AS EID, TO_CHAR(MAX(e.duration)) AS DUR FROM anc_per_abs_entries e JOIN per_all_people_f p ON p.person_id=e.person_id AND SYSDATE BETWEEN p.effective_start_date AND p.effective_end_date WHERE e.absence_type_id=300000071752546 AND e.start_date=DATE '${ABS_START_DASH}' AND p.person_number IN ('6','9','10') GROUP BY p.person_number
